text: PR #114 addresses 46 performance audit findings across 33 frontend files. Changes include: GPU-composited CSS transforms replacing layout-triggering properties, useMemo/memo/useCallback optimizations, next/dynamic lazy-loading, image loading optimizations, and corrected useState/useEffect patterns. One critical bug found in TierPathVisualizer where an absolutely-positioned progress bar loses its width. Several minor incomplete optimizations exist but are not regressions.

reasoning: Critical bug in TierPathVisualizer.tsx: absolutely-positioned progress bar has no explicit width, so transform: scaleX() produces a zero-width bar. Must fix before merge.

message: Correctly extracted inline ThemeToggleButton component and replaced with import from @/components/theme/ThemeToggle, eliminating the unmount/remount bug caused by defining a component inside another component.
Extracted VolunteerRow as a memoized component with stable callback props, preventing re-render of unchanged rows on any single checkbox/rating change.
Replaced blanket watch() (subscribes to ALL form fields) with individual field watches and scoped useMemo dependencies for preview date calculation.
Computed currentTier once before the map loop instead of O(n²) levels.find() inside levels.map(), improving render performance on the level path.
Replaced namespace import * as Sentry with named import { captureException }, enabling tree-shaking of unused Sentry exports.
Added useMemo for chartData derivation, preventing recharts re-render of all cells on every parent render.
Moved date formatting into useMemo (keyed on data) to avoid repeated new Date()/toLocaleDateString on every render for each transaction.
Added sizes prop to fill Image for responsive image loading in the 3-column grid, preventing full-width image download for ~33vw cards.
Converted static RichTextEditor import to next/dynamic with ssr:false, avoiding bundling @tiptap on pages that never open the blog form.
Extracted IconComponent constant before return statement, removing the IIFE that ran on every render.
Consolidated separate type-only and value imports from @/lib/shared into a single combined import statement.
Consolidated separate type-only and value imports from @/lib/shared into a single combined import statement.
Consolidated separate type-only and value imports from @/lib/shared into a single combined import statement.
Added duplicate guard (includes check) to addCertification to prevent adding blank or duplicate certifications.
Fixed React key from stat.label to `${stat.label}-${idx}` to prevent duplicate key collisions when stat labels repeat.
Extracted ToolbarButton into a memo() component and wrapped onUpdate handler in useCallback to reduce toolbar re-renders on every keystroke.
Changed staleTime from 0 to 30000 to match refetchInterval, preventing unnecessary refetch when cached data is still fresh.
Fixed stale closure by using onSuccess variables parameter instead of reviewMutation.variables, ensuring correct action label in the toast.

The progress fill div uses transform: scaleX() but is absolutely positioned with only left: 0 and no explicit width — an absolutely positioned contentless div has width 0, so scaleX(0.5) × 0 = 0, making the progress bar invisible. The old code used width: ${completionPct}% which correctly set the width relative to the parent container, but the new transform approach doesn't work without a base width.
suggestion: Add right-0 to the fill bar div so it stretches to full container width before scaleX reduces its visual width. Change className to include right-0: className="absolute top-1/2 left-0 right-0 h-1 -translate-y-1/2 bg-gradient-to-r from-brand-primary to-brand-secondary rounded-full origin-left transition-transform duration-700"

message: useMemo(() => allowedRoles, [allowedRoles]) is ineffective when parent components pass inline array literals (e.g. allowedRoles={['ADMIN']}), because the dependency reference changes every render. The effect still fires on every render, defeating the purpose of stabilization.
suggestion: Consider using individual role arguments via a rest parameter or document that parents must stabilize their allowedRoles with useMemo.

message: useEffect dependency changed from [volunteers] to [volunteers.length]. If an existing volunteer's properties change (e.g. checkedInAt updated after check-in sync) without the array length changing, local state won't resync with the new prop values. This is an intentional trade-off for performance but could cause stale UI.
suggestion: If prop mutations need to be reflected, consider using a hash of volunteer IDs or deep comparison. Document the intentional design choice.

message: TypeScript cast as EventSeriesFormData suppresses 8 missing fields from the partial object passed to calculatePreviewDates. If that function is later modified to access fields like startTime, endTime, venue, capacity, etc., the bug will be silent until runtime.
suggestion: Use Pick<EventSeriesFormData, 'frequency' | 'daysOfWeek' | 'interval' | 'firstEventDate' | 'endType' | 'maxOccurrences' | 'endDate'> instead of the blanket cast.

message: onChange prop passed to RichTextEditor is an inline arrow function (html) => setValue('content', html). This creates a new reference every render, which makes the useCallback in RichTextEditor.tsx:43 ineffective since handleUpdate depends on [onChange].
suggestion: Wrap the onChange prop in useCallback with stable dependencies, or use a ref to avoid recreating the callback.

message: Still uses namespace import import * as Sentry from '@sentry/nextjs' which prevents tree-shaking of unused Sentry exports. Only import { captureException } appears to be used.
suggestion: Replace with import { captureException } from '@sentry/nextjs' for consistency with the fix applied in ProofUploadForm.tsx.

message: htmlFor="recurring-toggle-active" targets a <div>, not a form element. The label click will not activate the toggle. Pre-existing accessibility issue.
suggestion: Use a proper checkbox/switch input element or apply role="switch" with aria-labelledby.

message: Uses relative imports (../../lib/api, ../../hooks/use-toast) instead of the @/ path alias used by the rest of the codebase (e.g. PointsHistory.tsx, ProtectedRoute.tsx, UserTable.tsx). Inconsistent with codebase convention.
suggestion: Use import { api } from '@/lib/api' for consistency.
